opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: (Call made by his partner) He woke up drenched in sweat, completely disoriented, and started shaking uncontrollably. I tried to give him juice, but he couldn't swallow it. He has diabetes and takes insulin. Please help — I don't know what to do.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: It came on suddenly over the last couple of hours and he's got worse in the last half hour — now he can't follow what I'm saying and can't drink anything.

symptom_narrative.onset: about two hours ago — sudden intense hunger and feeling very anxious, then heavy sweating and going pale
symptom_narrative.duration: roughly two hours, and much worse in the last 30 minutes
symptom_narrative.character: drenching sweat, pale and clammy, a fine shaking in both hands, heart racing; over the last half hour he's become confused and hard to rouse and now can't swallow
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the partner says he has never seen him this bad and he seems to be slipping away
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: his whole body — sweating, shaking, and now his mind is not right
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: came on fast and has kept getting worse without letting up
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: nothing — she tried orange juice but he couldn't drink it
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: it just keeps worsening on its own
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: he said his vision went double just before he became confused; he has been getting more drowsy and unresponsive
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: the partner thinks he has had milder 'hypos' before that he fixed himself with juice, but nothing like this
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: cannot sit up, follow commands, or drink safely; is drowsy and dependent

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: diabetes treated with insulin (partner says two kinds, a background one and a mealtime one)

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: partner is present and made the call; earlier this evening he took his usual mealtime insulin but skipped dinner because he felt a bit sick

objective_data_offered.value: 1.8 mmol/L on his home finger-prick glucose meter
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: single home glucometer reading taken by a distressed carer; not laboratory-confirmed

objective_data_offered.value: he is soaked in sweat with his clothing wet through, very pale, restless and only half awake, and cannot drink or speak clearly
both his hands are trembling and his heart feels like it is racing when I put my hand on his chest
a half-empty glass of orange juice is on the nightstand; his insulin pens are on the side table
